response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned, versatile engineer with roughly 15 years of experience, but his career centers on web and full-stack development in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. His hands-on C++ experience is limited to a seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and a bachelor thesis project, which falls far short of the deep, current C++/STL expertise this role demands. He also shows no exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443 secure development practices, MQTT, or the industrial protocols listed as advantageous, making him a poor fit despite strong general engineering craft.
strengths: Deep systems-programming experience with low-latency, high-throughput services (async I/O, fibers, caching) at Sociomantic, handling millions of requests per second
Strong software craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus a track record of mentoring via workshops, courses, and code katas
Experience building and operating event-driven, distributed platforms (GISCollective on Kubernetes) and open-source tooling
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and roughly a decade old; primary languages are TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang rather than modern C++/STL
No industrial automation, IoT-protocol (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), or IEC 62443 / SDL security background
Recent roles are web/product-focused full-stack work, distant from networked multi-threaded on-prem platform engineering the role requires

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity or IEC 62443 exposure
Strong systems and event-driven architecture fundamentals
